result.content: FINAL_ANSWER: [Implementation provided below] JUSTIFICATION: The implementation uses a greedy approach with a spatial grid to manage non-overlapping constraints, prioritizing advertisers based on their target area to maximize satisfaction score. Algorithmic Strategy: To solve the problem of placing non-overlapping axis-aligned rectangles in a 10000 x 10000 grid, we will use a Greedy Spatial Partitioning strategy. 1. Data Structures: Spatial Grid (coarse grid), Priority Queue (sorted by area descending), Interval Tree/Segment Tree. 2. Core Algorithm: Step 1: Sorting (descending area). Step 2: Iterative Placement (attempt rectangle centered at point, collision check via grid, expansion/contraction). Step 3: Refinement (local search/jitter).
